evidence_conflicts_detail: No material conflicts between steps. Step 2 post-hoc fragility and Step 3 replay fragility are directionally consistent. Old-branch REJECT for SM/LATCH was based on CAGR-weighted scoring, which is not a conflict with Step 3 finding them operationally robust — different questions.
final_manual_viability_assessment: SM only. No binary candidate is viable for manual or semi-manual deployment. SM loses 4% Sharpe at D4 (16h delay); binary candidates lose 29-41%.
sm_vs_latch_final_verdict: DROP LATCH. SM is preferred representative of vol-target cluster. Identical trade structure, identical replay fragility, lower complexity.
ema_overlay_final_verdict: EMA overlays add decision-relevant value under LT1. They increase baseline Sharpe and improve selectivity. Under LT2+, the added delay fragility makes the base variants (E0, E5) preferable. They deserve conditional deployment slots, not unconditional preference.
binary_latency_requirement: LT1 (<4h) for full performance. LT2 (4-16h) acceptable with 17-31% Sharpe degradation. LT3 (>16h) NOT VIABLE for any binary candidate.
step5_necessity_verdict: NOT NEEDED for research conclusion. RECOMMENDED for live sign-off hardening. Step 5 could NOT realistically change candidate ranking — it would only refine confidence bounds around delay sensitivity and validate the replay harness against the live engine.
recommended_next_step: Step 5 (optional): live-engine replay validation and combined-disruption stress testing for deployment confidence.
